jjgtn_text: ## Character
Synthesis and writing. Distill the design conversation into two complementary documents with different audiences and lifespans.
## Deliverables
**APCS-Specification.md (Product Spec):**
- Full UX plan: focus-to-consume workflow, three-category triage (red/yellow/grey), findings panel with ELIDE/PASS toggles, "Copy Anonymized" button, DOB→age transform
- Self-update behavior description (user-facing: app updates itself silently)
- Deployment model (scp to shared directory, Ann launches independently)
- Target platforms (macOS first, Windows planned)
- Open Evidence context and HIPAA Safe Harbor motivation
- Pipeline/detection section: deliberately blank, marked with note pointing to APCPS as the living engineering document
- Redistribution considerations (public domain dictionaries, SNOMED/NLM deferred)
**APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md (Prototype Spec):**
- Full three-tier detection pipeline: regex → label-anchored → dictionary
- Tier 1 regex patterns enumerated (SSN, phone, date, email, address, zip, labeled identifiers)
- Tier 2 Epic label vocabulary (Patient:, Attending:, Facility:, etc.)
- Tier 3 dictionary strategy: blacklist/whitelist, collision resolution, curated-small sizing
- Anonymization strategy: category-specific placeholders, DOB→age transform, plain text output
- Tech stack with crate choices and rationale
- Project structure with full file tree and prefix tree
- Scope boundaries: no persistence, one patient, no configuration, no click-to-highlight
- NO UX plan (lives in APCS only)
## Approach
Read the paddock thoroughly — it captures the full design conversation. Translate into spec prose. Ensure both documents cross-reference each other at their boundary.
## Character
Mechanical with minting attention. Create every file and directory, wire BUK dispatch, get first Tauri window on screen.
## Deliverables
- `Tools/apck/` directory with full tree per APCPS project structure section
- `apcw_workbench.sh` — routes `apcw-*` commands
- `apcz_zipper.sh` — enrolls colophons with BUK dispatch
- `apck-claude-context.md` — already exists from ₢A9AAA; review and update if needed
- `apcd/Cargo.toml` — two `[[bin]]` targets: `apcap` (Tauri app), `apcal` (fixture loader)
- `apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` — Tauri entry point, launches window with static "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" title and initial instruction text
- `apcd/src/lib.rs` — module declarations (empty stubs) and test module declarations per APCPS:
- Source: `apcre_engine`, `apcrp_parse`, `apcrm_match`, `apcrd_dictionaries`, `apcru_update`
- Tests: `#[cfg(test)]` for `apcte_engine`, `apctp_parse`, `apctm_match`, `apctd_dictionaries`
- Empty stub files for all source and test modules
- `apcd/ui/index.html`, `style.css`, `app.js` — minimal Tauri frontend showing initial state
- Tabtargets: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`, `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh`, `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`, `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh`, `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh`
- `test_fixtures/` directory (empty, ready for next pace)
- `apcd/dictionaries/` directory (empty, ready for later pace)
- Tauri config (`tauri.conf.json` or Cargo-based Tauri 2.x config)
## Verification
- `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` launches a Tauri window displaying "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" with instruction text
- `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` produces a `.app` bundle
- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` runs cargo test (passes trivially — no tests yet)
## Character
Integration work — two independent pieces (fixture loader + clipboard consumption) that meet in the middle.
## Deliverables
**Fixtures (DONE):**

- `test_fixtures/epic_progress_note.html` — synthetic Epic "Copy All" HTML with full PHI coverage across 15 of 18 HIPAA categories. Demographics header, narrative HPI with embedded names/addresses, vitals, labs, medications, assessment/plan, signature block.

- `test_fixtures/epic_geriatric_consult.html` — geriatric consult exercising the age >89 rule (DOB 11/02/1932, age 93 must emit "Age: 90+"). Lighter PHI footprint, cognitive assessment scores that must survive, brand/generic medication pairs.
**Fixture loader (`apcd/src/apcal_main.rs`):**
- Uses `arboard` to write HTML file contents to clipboard as HTML format
- Takes fixture file path as argument
**Tabtargets (imprint channel — one per fixture):**
- `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` — loads `epic_progress_note.html` onto clipboard
- `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh` — loads `epic_geriatric_consult.html` onto clipboard
- Colophon `apcw-fl`, channel `imprint`
**Clipboard consumption (`apcap_main.rs` update):**
- On Tauri window focus, read clipboard HTML via `arboard::get_html()`, fall back to `get_text()`
- Display raw clipboard content in the webview
- Store last consumed clipboard content for change detection — on refocus, compare byte-for-byte; if unchanged, preserve current display without reprocessing
Note: Clinical content heuristic and clipboard clearing are deferred to A9AAE (detection engine), where the heuristic logic naturally lives. This pace implements raw consume-and-display.
## Verification

Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh`, then focus the Tauri app — raw Epic HTML content appears in the window. Repeat with `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh`. Refocus away and back — display preserved, no reprocessing.
## Character
Algorithmic — transform semi-structured HTML into a representation the detection engine can consume.
## Deliverables
- `apcd/src/apcrp_parse.rs` — Parse Epic HTML using `scraper`. Extract:
- Labeled fields: identify `Label: value` patterns, emit (label, value) pairs
- Section boundaries: detect Epic section headers (Chief Complaint, HPI, PMH, Medications, Vitals, Labs, A/P, Signature)
- Freetext runs: narrative text between labels/sections
- Preserve source position information so findings can map back to the original text for rendering
- Define data structures for parsed output: a sequence of typed spans (LabeledField, SectionHeader, Narrative, etc.) each carrying their text content and source offset
- Plain text extraction path: strip all HTML, preserve line breaks and section structure
- `apcd/src/apctp_parse.rs` — unit tests: feed synthetic fixture HTML through parser, assert correct extraction of labeled fields, section boundaries, and narrative spans
## Verification
- Unit tests pass via `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh`

- Feed the synthetic fixture through the parser. Print structured output showing detected labels, sections, and narrative spans with positions. All labeled PHI fields (Patient, DOB, MRN, Attending, Facility, etc.) correctly identified as LabeledField spans.
## Character

The intellectual core. Data acquisition (done — dictionary files committed), algorithm implementation, and clinical content gating. Requires attention to the anchored/matched vocabulary distinction and case normalization.
## Deliverables
**Dictionaries (DONE):**
- `dictionaries/surnames.txt` — 1,000 US surnames (Census 2010 approximation)
- `dictionaries/firstnames.txt` — 1,000 US first names (SSA approximation)
- `dictionaries/cities.txt` — 134 US state capitals + top cities
- `dictionaries/medical_whitelist.txt` — 1,058 curated medical terms
- `dictionaries/english_whitelist.txt` — 5,000 common English words
- All lowercase, sorted, deduplicated. Verified against test fixtures.
**`apcd/src/apcrd_dictionaries.rs`:**
- Load dictionary files at startup
- Build `aho-corasick` automaton from lowercase blacklist entries (surnames + firstnames + cities)
- Build whitelist hash sets from lowercase medical + English terms
- Case-insensitive lookups: input tokens lowercased before matching, original case preserved for display
- Expose lookup API: is_blacklisted, is_whitelisted, classify_word
**`apcd/src/apcrm_match.rs`:**

- Tier 1 regex patterns: SSN, phone, email, dates (multiple formats), street addresses, zip codes, MRN/account/encounter following labels. Known ambiguities accepted (zip matching 5-digit numbers, date matching version numbers) — conservative bias, clinician reviews.

- Tier 2 label-anchored extraction: per-word anchoring — each word following a known Epic label is individually flagged RED. These are **anchored** items, independent of any dictionary. Anchored classification is final.
- Tier 3 dictionary scan: aho-corasick pass with case-insensitive matching, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW. These are **matched** items.
**`apcd/src/apcre_engine.rs`:**
- Orchestrate: take parsed spans from `apcrp`, run all three tiers, merge by highest severity (anchored RED > matched YELLOW > PASS)

- Output: classified token stream — each token carries its text, classification (RED/YELLOW/PASS), mechanism (anchored/matched/regex), PHI type label (NAME, DATE, MRN, PHONE, etc.), and source position
- DOB→age transform: when a date is labeled DOB, compute age from current date. Ages >89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor (45 CFR 164.514(b)(2)(i)(C)).

- Clinical content heuristic: scan for 2+ known clinical labels (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.) to gate processing. Non-clinical content returns a diagnostic (content length, type, first ~100 chars) for debugging. Clipboard cleared after successful consumption.
**Unit tests:**
- `apctd_dictionaries.rs` — dictionary loading, case-insensitive lookup, blacklist/whitelist/collision classification
- `apctm_match.rs` — regex patterns against known examples (SSN, phone, dates, addresses), label-anchored per-word extraction, anchored vs matched precedence
- `apcte_engine.rs` — full pipeline with synthetic fixture: all PHI caught, all medical terms pass, DOB→age with >89 handling, clinical heuristic acceptance/rejection
## Verification
- All unit tests pass via `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh`

- Feed synthetic fixture through full pipeline (parse → detect → classify). Print findings list showing every detected PHI item with tier (anchored/matched/regex), severity, category label, and matched text. All names, dates, identifiers, addresses, phones in the fixture caught. Medical terms, medications, lab values, vitals pass clean.
## Character
UX implementation. HTML/CSS/JS frontend work in the Tauri webview, wired to Rust backend results via Tauri IPC.
## Deliverables
- `apcd/ui/index.html` updated: three states:
1. **Initial state**: "Ann's PHI Clipbuddy" title + "Copy a patient note from Epic, then switch to this window" instruction
2. **Non-clinical clipboard**: diagnostic line (content length, type, first ~100 chars), returns to instruction state
3. **Triage view**: two-pane layout — document preview (top/left) and findings panel (bottom/right)
- `apcd/ui/style.css` updated: RED (`background: #ff000030; border: 2px solid red`), YELLOW (`background: #ffff0030; border: 2px solid orange`), GREY (`color: #999`) inline highlight styles
- `apcd/ui/app.js` updated: receive classified token stream from Rust backend via Tauri `invoke`, render document preview with inline highlights, populate findings panel
- Tauri IPC commands: `consume_clipboard` (triggered on window focus), returns classified token stream as JSON or non-clinical diagnostic
- Findings panel with **two sections**:
- **Questionable Findings** (upper, YELLOW): dictionary matches and ambiguous detections. Default ELIDE, easily toggled to PASS.
- **Definite PHI** (lower, RED): label-anchored and regex-matched identifiers. Default ELIDE, toggleable to PASS but separated to create deliberate friction.
- Each entry shows: matched text, PHI category label, [ELIDE ▼] / [PASS ▼] toggle
- Toggle changes update the document preview in real time
- Clipboard change detection on refocus: unchanged clipboard preserves triage state
## Verification

Load fixture → focus app → see the synthetic Epic note rendered with names in red boxes, questionable words in yellow boxes, medical terms and clinical content in grey. YELLOW findings in upper panel section, RED in lower. Toggle a yellow finding to PASS — preview updates. Toggle a red finding to PASS — requires scrolling to lower section. Refocus without clipboard change — triage preserved. Visual confirmation against the synthetic fixture that all PHI is highlighted and all clinical content passes clean.
## Character
Pipeline completion. Wire the "Copy Anonymized" button to produce clean text from the triage state.
## Deliverables
- `apcre_engine.rs` updated: generate anonymized plain text from classified token stream respecting current ELIDE/PASS state
- Category-specific placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[DATE]`, `[MRN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[SSN]`, `[FACILITY]`, `[PROVIDER]`, `[ACCOUNT]`, `[ENCOUNTER_ID]`
- DOB→age transform: dates labeled as DOB emit `Age: NN` instead of `[DATE]`. Ages >89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor.
- PASS items (including RED items toggled to PASS by clinician): emit original text unchanged
- ELIDE items: emit placeholder for their PHI category
- Preserve line breaks and section structure from parsed output — plain text should be readable, not a wall
- Tauri IPC command: `copy_anonymized` — takes current toggle state from frontend, generates clean text, writes to clipboard via `arboard::set_text()`
- Frontend: "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" button at bottom of window, calls `copy_anonymized`
## Verification

Load fixture → triage view appears → toggle a few yellow items to PASS → toggle one red item to PASS → click "Copy Anonymized" → paste into a text editor. Verify: all ELIDE items replaced with category placeholders, DOB replaced with computed age (90+ for >89), toggled PASS items preserved as original text, section structure readable, no PHI leaks. Text should be suitable for pasting into Open Evidence.
## Character
Infrastructure and platform integration. Two independent pieces: self-update mechanism in the app, and the deploy tabtarget that ships to Ann's machine.
## Deliverables
**Self-update (`apcd/src/apcru_update.rs`):**
- Watch `/Users/Shared/apcua/` using `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS)
- On new `.app` bundle detected: copy over current app bundle, spawn `open -n` on new path, exit current process
- No confirmation dialog — fully automatic
- Graceful handling: if watch directory doesn't exist, create it silently and keep watching
- Run watcher on a background thread, independent of main Tauri event loop
**Deploy tabtarget (`tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh`):**
- `cargo tauri build` (release)
- `scp` the `.app` bundle to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/`
- Report success with bundle size and path
**Integration:**
- Wire `apcru_update` into `apcap_main.rs` — start watcher on app launch
- Update `apck-claude-context.md` with deploy workflow documentation
## Verification
1. Build and run app locally. Confirm watcher starts (log message).
2. Copy a dummy `.app` to `/Users/Shared/apcua/` — app detects it (may not self-replace locally since paths differ, but detection fires).
3. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` completes: bundle appears on Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`.
4. End-to-end: Ann launches app, we deploy an update, app auto-restarts with new version.
## Character

Mechanical data engineering. Replace LLM-approximated dictionary files with real public-domain data via download-and-transform script. Quality improvement, not functional change — the detection engine already works with current files.
## Deliverables
**Dictionary acquisition command (via `apcc_cli.sh` or dedicated tabtarget):**
- Shell script that downloads and transforms public-domain sources into dictionary files
- Surnames: Census 2010 `Names_2010Census.csv` → top N by frequency, lowercase, sorted
- First names: SSA baby names (national data) → top N by cumulative frequency across decades, lowercase, sorted
- Cities: Census gazetteer or equivalent → state capitals + top 100 cities, lowercase, sorted
- English whitelist: established word frequency list (e.g., `google-10000-english` or similar) → top 5000+, lowercase, sorted
- Medical whitelist: untouched (LLM-curated, clinical judgment required)
- Output replaces files in `apcd/dictionaries/`. Idempotent — safe to re-run.
- Document source URLs and licenses in a comment header or companion README
## Verification
- Run the script — dictionary files regenerated from real sources
- File format unchanged (one-per-line, lowercase, sorted, no duplicates)
- Existing unit tests still pass with new dictionary content
- Spot-check: Census top 10 surnames present, SSA top 10 first names present
## Character
Mechanical Rust implementation + execution debugging. All research and algorithm design is complete — this pace is pure coding from a known spec, then running and fixing.
## Context

Prior pace (A9AAI) researched and verified all data sources, designed transform pipelines, and decided on Rust over shell (CSV quoting fragility in awk was the decisive factor). This docket captures everything needed to implement without rediscovery.
## Deliverables
### New binary: `apcad` (App Dictionary)
New file: `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs`
Prefix `apcad` under existing `apca` non-terminal (alongside `apcal` Loader, `apcap` Prototype).
Add `[[bin]] name = "apcad"` to `Tools/apck/apcd/Cargo.toml`.
New crate dependencies: `csv`, `zip` (for reading zip archives from bytes), `ureq` (lightweight sync HTTP — no async needed).
### Tabtarget enrollment
- New tabtarget: `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` — runs `cargo run --bin apcad` in apcd/
- New command in `apcc_cli.sh`: `apcc_dictionary_refresh`
- Enroll in `apcz_zipper.sh`: colophon `apcw-dr`, no folio
### Data sources and transforms
All sources are public domain or CC-BY 3.0. All URLs verified live as of 2026-04-10.
**1. Surnames — Census 2010** (top 1000)
- URL: `https://www2.census.gov/topics/genealogy/2010surnames/names.zip`
- Inside zip: `Names_2010Census.csv`
- CSV with header: `name,rank,count,prop100k,cum_prop100k,pctwhite,pctblack,pctapi,pctaian,pct2prace,pcthispanic`
- Names are ALL CAPS — lowercase on output
- File is pre-ranked by frequency — take first 1000 data rows by column `name`
- License: CC0 / US government public domain
**2. First names — SSA Baby Names** (top 1000)
- URL: `https://www.ssa.gov/oact/babynames/names.zip`
- Inside zip: `yob1880.txt` through `yob2024.txt` (no headers), plus `NationalReadMe.pdf`
- Per-file format: `Name,Sex,Count` (e.g., `Mary,F,7065`)
- Aggregation: HashMap by lowercase name (merge M+F), sum counts across all year files, sort desc by total count, take top 1000

- **SSA uses Akamai bot protection — curl may get 403.** Handle gracefully: attempt download, on failure print instructions for manual download to a staging path, exit with clear message. Re-run should detect the pre-staged file.
- License: CC0 / US government public domain
**3. Cities — Census Population Estimates** (top 100 by pop + 50 state capitals)
- URL: `https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest/datasets/2020-2023/cities/totals/sub-est2023.csv`
- Use `csv` crate with headers — access columns by name, not position
- Filter: `SUMLEV` == `162` (incorporated places)
- Sort by `POPESTIMATE2023` descending, take top 100
- Strip municipal suffixes from `NAME`: " city", " town", " village", " borough", " municipality", " CDP" (case-insensitive, trailing)
- Remove surrounding quotes if present
- Merge with hardcoded 50 state capitals (below)
- Deduplicate, lowercase, alpha-sort
- Expected output: ~130 entries
- License: US government public domain
**4. English whitelist — Norvig/Google** (top 5000)
- URL: `https://norvig.com/ngrams/count_1w.txt`
- TSV format: `word\tcount`, 333K lines, pre-sorted by frequency desc
- Filter: skip entries with digits, skip entries shorter than 2 chars
- Take first 5000 that pass filter, lowercase, alpha-sort
- License: CC-BY 3.0 (Google n-gram corpus)
**5. Medical whitelist — UNTOUCHED**
- Do not modify `medical_whitelist.txt`
### 50 US State Capitals (hardcode in source)
albany, annapolis, atlanta, augusta, austin,
baton rouge, bismarck, boise, boston, carson city,
charleston, cheyenne, columbia, columbus, concord,
denver, des moines, dover, frankfort, harrisburg,
hartford, helena, honolulu, indianapolis, jackson,
jefferson city, juneau, lansing, lincoln, little rock,
madison, montgomery, montpelier, nashville, oklahoma city,
olympia, phoenix, pierre, providence, raleigh,
richmond, sacramento, salem, salt lake city, santa fe,
springfield, st. paul, tallahassee, topeka, trenton
Note: current `cities.txt` uses "saint paul" (line 107) but "st. louis" / "st. petersburg" (lines 121-122). Normalize to match whatever the Census CSV uses after suffix stripping.
### Output format
Each file: one entry per line, lowercase, alphabetically sorted (`sort -u` equivalent), no trailing blank lines. File ends with a single newline after the last entry. This matches the current format.
Output directory: `Tools/apck/apcd/dictionaries/`
### After implementation
- Build: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` (or just `cargo build` in apcd/)
- Run: `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` (or `cargo run --bin apcad` in apcd/)
- Verify output format matches current files (one-per-line, lowercase, sorted)
- Spot-check: smith/johnson/williams in surnames, james/mary/john in firstnames, all 50 capitals in cities
- Run tests: `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` — fix any failures from changed dictionary content
- Confirm idempotent: second run produces identical output
- Update `apck-claude-context.md`: add APCAD to file acronym mappings and prefix tree
## Verification
- `cargo build --bin apcad` succeeds
- `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` runs end-to-end (or with manual SSA download workaround)
- Four dictionary files regenerated from real sources
- `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` passes clean
- Script is idempotent (second run = identical output)
## Character

Guided manual verification. No code changes expected — this is a structured test session using the two synthetic fixtures to confirm the full pipeline works end-to-end on the developer's machine. Debugging character: if something fails, diagnose and fix inline.
## Prerequisites
- App builds clean: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` or `cargo build` in apcd/
- Two fixture loaders available: `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` and `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh`
## Walkthrough — Progress Note Fixture
1. Run `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` to launch the app
2. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` to load progress note HTML onto clipboard
3. Switch to the app window — triage view should appear
4. Confirm: RED findings include Patient (Margaret Thornton), Attending (Dr. Sarah Whitfield), DOB date, SSN, MRN, phone, email, address, URL
5. Confirm: YELLOW findings include dictionary-matched names in narrative
6. Confirm: DOB shows the date highlighted, not an age yet (age appears in anonymized output)
7. Toggle two YELLOW items to PASS — confirm preview updates (grey styling)
8. Toggle one RED item to PASS — confirm preview updates
9. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" — button should show "Copied!"
10. Paste into a text editor. Verify:
- Elided items show category placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[SSN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[ADDRESS]`, etc.
- DOB shows `Age: 74` (or appropriate computed age)
- Items toggled to PASS show original text
- Line breaks and section structure preserved — readable, not a wall
- No PHI leaks in elided regions
## Walkthrough — Geriatric Consult Fixture
1. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh` to load geriatric HTML onto clipboard
2. Switch to app — triage view should refresh with new content
3. Confirm: clinical content detected, findings populated
4. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" with all defaults (everything elided)
5. Paste and verify: all PHI replaced, structure preserved
6. If geriatric fixture has a DOB for patient >89 years old, confirm `Age: 90+` appears
## Edge Cases to Probe
- Refocus without changing clipboard — should show "unchanged", triage preserved
- Copy non-clinical text (e.g., "hello world") — should show non-clinical diagnostic, triage cleared
- After non-clinical, click "Copy Anonymized" — should show error (no clinical data)
- Refocus with original fixture again — full triage should reappear
## Exit Criteria
All walkthrough steps pass on both fixtures. Any bugs found are fixed and notched inline before wrapping.
## Character

Field enablement. Ship a harvest-capable build to Ann, confirm captures accumulate on first real Epic use, and establish the feedback cadence. This is the trigger that starts fixture collection — not a validation milestone for the current detection engine.
## Prerequisites
- `epic-capture-harvest` (₢A9AAR) complete; harvest mechanism live in `apcap_main.rs` and APCPS `Clipboard Harvest` section merged.
- `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` clean on developer machine.
## Deploy
1. Build release: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`.
2. Deploy: `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` (scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/`).
3. Self-update watcher picks up the new bundle on next focus; manual launch if app not running.
## First Real Capture Session
1. On Ann's machine, open Epic, navigate to a patient chart.
2. Epic "Copy All" to copy a clinical note.
3. Switch to PHI Clipbuddy — triage view appears.
4. Confirm by listing: `ls -la $HOME/apck_harvest/` — expect at least one `10000.{html,txt}` pair (and any additional flavors the clipboard abstraction surfaced).
5. Process a second note — expect `10001.*` to appear.
## What to Observe (not validate)
- Flavor inventory: which extensions actually land? HTML always? Plain text always? RTF ever? Image ever?
- HTML shape: is Epic's HTML similar to the synthetic fixtures, or structurally different?
- Detection quality: RED/YELLOW categorization on real content — note misses and overcatches, but do NOT fix inline. Capture observations for the next iteration.
## Cadence Establishment
- Ann continues to use the app during normal Epic workflow.
- Captures accumulate in `$HOME/apck_harvest/` — outside any git tree, manually reviewed.
- User (developer) coordinates anonymization and fixture promotion out-of-band.
## Exit Criteria
- At least two real Epic captures written to `$HOME/apck_harvest/` on Ann's machine with both HTML and text flavors present.
- Flavor inventory noted (which extensions appeared).
- First observations about Epic's real HTML shape recorded — paddock discussion or a follow-up itch is acceptable.
- Ann comfortable with the capture workflow; no further deploy needed for routine use.
## Out of Scope
- Detection quality tuning (next iteration, once anonymized fixtures exist).
- Anonymization procedure definition (user coordinates manually).
- Open Evidence paste validation (may happen, but not the gating deliverable).
- False-positive / false-negative scoring (needs anonymized fixtures first).
## Character
Interactive design decision. The premise is clear and the options are enumerated — this pace is a conversation to pick a path, then a small implementation.
## Problem

The `apcad` dictionary refresh binary cannot download SSA Baby Names data programmatically. SSA uses Akamai bot protection that 403s non-browser HTTP clients (ureq, curl). The current workaround requires a human to download `names.zip` in a browser and place it at a staging path (`.ssa_names_staging.zip`). This builds a human into a loop that should be automatable.
## Options Identified

1. **Commit the staging zip** (~7.7MB, public domain). Tool already prefers staging file over download. Eliminates human-in-loop entirely. Update by committing a new zip whenever new year data matters.
2. **Use an alternative mirror** (GitHub, Kaggle, data.gov). Avoids Akamai but trades URL stability.
3. **Browser-mimicking HTTP** (curl-impersonate, crafted TLS). Arms race with Akamai.
4. **Headless browser** (chromiumoxide, playwright). Heavy dependency for one file.
5. **Accept the status quo** — staging zip is a one-time manual step that persists indefinitely. SSA data is historical; new year files are annual.
## Deliverables
- Interactive decision on which option to pursue
- Implement the chosen resolution
- Remove or update the staging fallback messaging if no longer needed
## Character

Focused detection enhancement. Mechanical implementation following established Tier 2/3 patterns — no design ambiguity. The aho-corasick infrastructure already exists for Tier 3; this reuses it with a dynamic phrase dictionary built per-document.
## Problem

Tier 2 (label-anchored) identifies entities like "Maine Medical Center" from the `Facility:` field, but when the same phrase appears in narrative text, no tier catches it. Tier 3 (dictionary) operates on individual words, and common words like "Medical" and "Center" are whitelisted. The identifying unit is the multi-word phrase, not the individual tokens.
## Approach
Add entity propagation between Tier 2 and Tier 3 in `apcre_engine.rs`:
1. After `apcrm_scan_anchored` completes, collect the full multi-word values from labeled field spans (Facility, Patient, Attending, Provider, Referring, etc.)
2. Build a case-insensitive aho-corasick automaton from those phrases
3. Scan `plain_text` for matches — emit RED findings with a new `Propagated` mechanism variant
4. Existing merge logic (`zapcre_merge_findings`) handles overlap with Tier 2's original findings
**Whitespace normalization**: Already handled by the parser (`zapcrp_normalize_whitespace`) — both `plain_text` and span values are single-space-normalized. No additional normalization needed.
**Category**: Inherit the category from the originating Tier 2 finding (Facility stays Facility, Provider stays Provider, etc.)
## Scope
- Modify `apcrm_match.rs`: add `Propagated` to `apcrm_Mechanism` enum
- Modify `apcre_engine.rs`: add propagation scan between Tier 2 and Tier 3 calls
- Add unit tests for propagation (facility in narrative, provider in signature block)
- No frontend changes needed — findings render the same regardless of mechanism

## Deliverables
New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.

- Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
- Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
- No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)

Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

## Character

Focused refactoring with a policy enforcement dimension. Mechanically moving rendering logic from JS to Rust string-building — the HTML structure and CSS stay, only the generation language changes. The policy part (documenting the no-JS rule) is small but must not be forgotten.
## Problem

The JS frontend creates a language boundary (Rust ↔ JS) that produces gratuitous bugs like the byte-vs-char offset mismatch fixed in ₢A9AAK. JavaScript adds a second type system, a second set of string semantics, and a serialization boundary that exists only because the prototype chose the path of least resistance. For a HIPAA-adjacent tool that processes PHI, minimizing boundary surface is a correctness concern, not just aesthetics.
## Approach — Option A (Tauri + Rust-rendered HTML)
Keep Tauri's webview as a dumb renderer. Generate complete HTML in Rust for every state change. Remove all JS files.

1. **Move focus handling to Rust**: Use Tauri's `WindowEvent::Focused` on the Rust side instead of JS `window.addEventListener("focus")`. On focus, run the existing `consume_clipboard` logic, then push rendered HTML to the webview.

2. **Build HTML renderer in Rust**: Port the 5 JS rendering functions (`zapcapHandleResult`, `zapcapShowTriage`, `zapcapShowNonClinical`, `zapcapRenderPreview`, `zapcapRenderFindings`) into Rust functions that produce complete HTML strings. The byte offsets from the engine are consumed directly — no char-offset conversion needed. Delete `zapcap_byte_to_char_offsets`.
3. **Handle toggle state in Rust**: Toggle clicks invoke a Tauri command (e.g., `toggle_finding(index)`) that flips state in Rust and pushes re-rendered HTML. Minimal IPC: just an index integer.

4. **Handle copy in Rust**: `copy_anonymized` command already exists; the button just needs to call it (one tiny inline `onclick` handler calling `window.__TAURI__.core.invoke` is acceptable as a Tauri webview primitive, or use Tauri's event system).
5. **Delete JS**: Remove `app.js` entirely. `index.html` becomes a minimal shell that receives rendered content. `style.css` stays.
## Policy Documentation Updates
Update these locations to prohibit JavaScript in the APCK codebase:
- **APCPS (Prototype Specification)** — Add a premise: "No JavaScript. All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface." This is the authoritative technical constraint.
- **apck-claude-context.md** — Add to the conventions section so future Claude sessions see it immediately.
- **APCS (Product Specification)** — Note in the tech stack section that the frontend is Rust-rendered HTML, not a JS application.
- **Heat paddock** — Note the rationale (byte/char boundary bug class elimination) so it's visible during this heat's remaining work.
## Exit Criteria
- `app.js` deleted from the repository
- All three UI states (instruction, non-clinical, triage) render correctly from Rust
- Toggle buttons work via Tauri command → Rust state → re-render cycle
- Copy Anonymized works as before
- `zapcap_byte_to_char_offsets` deleted (no longer needed)
- All four documents updated with no-JS policy
- Existing tests pass (`tt/apcw-t.Test.sh`)
## Character

Exploratory engineering spike, not mechanical implementation. A parallel investigation that does NOT touch `apcre_engine.rs` — builds a standalone binary that runs the StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base model locally via ONNX Runtime and emits `.stanford.assay.txt` alongside the existing `apcab` `.assay.txt` on the same fixtures for side-by-side comparison.

Multiple unknowns to surface early: the model's BIO label set and its semantic overlap with APCK categories, token-to-byte offset alignment through the tokenizer, macOS ORT bundling implications if ever productized, and whether a ~440MB neural model earns its footprint versus the current 3-tier engine. The spike's deliverable is comparative evidence, not integration.

**Anticipated hazard**: token-to-byte offset alignment is the same bug class that drove ₢A9AAP (byte-vs-char mismatch in the JS frontend). The Rust `tokenizers` crate returns offsets against whatever string you tokenized — ensure the plain text whose offsets get reported is byte-identical to the string fed to the tokenizer, and decide up front whether byte or char units are authoritative.
## Goal

Local PHI de-identification on macOS using StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base exported to ONNX, loaded via the `ort` crate, and compared against the current 3-tier engine on the existing HTML fixtures.
## Architecture Decisions
- **Parallel binary `apcnsa`** (App Neural Stanford Assay), mirrors `apcab`. No engine integration.
- **Output format**: same guillemet markers as `apcab`, distinct suffix `{stem}.stanford.assay.txt` so both binaries can emit into `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` without collision.
- **Raw stanford labels** in the guillemets (e.g. `‹NAME Margaret Thornton›`, `‹DATE 03/15/1952›`). No mapping to APCK categories in this pace — mapping is a followup if the spike is promising.

- **Model artifact policy**: hardcoded absolute path `/Users/bhyslop/models/stanford-deidentifier/` holds `model.onnx`, `tokenizer.json`, `vocab.txt`, `config.json`. Written as a constant `ZAPCC_STANFORD_DIR` in `apcc_cli.sh`. Distribution policy deferred — decide only if the spike earns productization.
- **Python export as a workbench function**, not a `scripts/` file. Matches APCK's flat layout and BCG's function-over-script-file preference.

- **Subtree prefix `apcns*`** declared for neural-stanford work, with `a` classifier for the binary → `apcnsa`. Mirrors `apca*`/`apcr*`/`apct*` convention. Future neural sources would be `apcnsr_*`, tests `apcnst_*`. Terminal exclusivity verified against the current prefix tree (`apcn*` is unused).

## Problem

Clipboard is consumed destructively — `get_text()` runs, detection runs, then the system clipboard is zeroed. We have no understanding of Epic's real flavor structure, and we can never grow the fixture library from real traffic.
## Solution
Before the clipboard zero-out on the `Clinical` branch, write every arboard-accessible flavor verbatim to a numbered capture in `$HOME/apck_harvest/`.
## Deliverables — Code

- New module `apcrh_harvest.rs` exposing `pub fn apcrh_capture_all_flavors(dir: &Path) -> Result<u32, String>`; internal helpers `zapcrh_scan_next_index`, `zapcrh_write_text`, `zapcrh_write_html` (whichever flavors the crate version of arboard exposes).
- Constants:
- `pub const APCRH_HARVEST_SEED_INDEX: u32 = 10000;`
- `pub const APCRH_HARVEST_DIR_NAME: &str = "apck_harvest";`
- Path is `$HOME/apck_harvest/` (no dotfile prefix). Resolve `$HOME` via `std::env::var("HOME")`.
- Test file `apcth_harvest.rs`:
- Empty directory → returns `APCRH_HARVEST_SEED_INDEX` (10000).
- Directory with `10000.html` and `10000.txt` present → next index is 10001 (same-index group counted as one).
- Non-numeric filenames present → ignored in max calculation.
- Gaps in numeric sequence → max+1, not lowest-gap-fill.
- `lib.rs` wires `pub mod apcrh_harvest;` and `#[cfg(test)] mod apcth_harvest;`.

- `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`: on `apcre_Result::Clinical`, before `clipboard.set_text(String::new())`, call `apcrh_capture_all_flavors(&harvest_dir)`. Failure logs via `apcrl_error_now!` and does NOT abort triage. Directory created lazily via `std::fs::create_dir_all`.
- `apck-claude-context.md`: add `APCRH` file-acronym mapping, add `apcrh` node under `apcr*` in the prefix tree.
- `.gitignore`: add `apck_harvest/` as belt-and-suspenders against accidental repo-tree placement.
## Deliverables — Specification
New section in `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` titled `Clipboard Harvest` specifying:
- *Purpose*: preserve verbatim real-world clipboard contents on Clinical-branch detection to grow the fixture library and understand Epic's flavor structure.
- *Trigger*: the Clinical branch of clipboard analysis, executed before the system-clipboard zero-out.
- *Storage*: `$HOME/apck_harvest/` — outside any repo tree; directory created lazily.
- *Naming*: `{N}.{ext}` where `N` seeds at 10000 for empty directories, otherwise `max_existing_N + 1`. Same `N` groups all flavors of one capture.
- *Flavors*: every flavor the clipboard abstraction exposes (text, HTML at minimum; RTF and image documented as gaps if the abstraction does not surface them).
- *Failure mode*: a capture failure logs through the standard error channel and does not abort triage — user-visible behavior is unchanged.
- *Privacy posture*: PHI-at-rest stays outside the repo; anonymization and promotion to `test_fixtures/` are manual, out-of-band.
## Constraints
- No new crate dependencies. arboard is already in the tree.
- RCG conformance: `apcrh_` prefix (verified unused, terminal exclusive), `pub(crate)` + z-prefix for internal items, output via `apcrl_*_now!` only, `deny(warnings)` preserved.

## Character

Mechanical application of settled decisions. The substantive design work happened in the session that produced this pace — every decision below is a write-down, not an open question. Sonnet-scale: judgment calls are narrow (prefix alignment, linked-term phrasing, quoin placement), but the decisions themselves need no re-deliberation.
## Context
The Stanford spike (₢A9AAQ) exposed structural incoherence in APCS0. A design conversation reshaped the concept model along these lines:
- **Byte ranges are implementation details, not vocabulary.** The concept model talks about regions of normalized text; byte offsets live in code only.
- **Algorithms run in parallel on the same input**, not serially refining each other's output. Each discerner is a pure function of `apcs_normalized_text`.
- **Severity vocabulary (RED/YELLOW/PASS) is dropped.** It conflated epistemic confidence, default disposition, and display affordance — three distinct things collapsed under one label.

- **No generic `apcs_algorithm` or `apcs_reliability_profile`.** Discerners are named intimately; reconciliation rules reference them by name. Generic abstractions were premature and invited non-load-bearing complexity.
- **Four concrete discerners**, each with its own registry/parameter quoin and its own finding-output quoin, shaped in parallel but intimately named (no common supertype).
- **`apcs_reconciliation` is named** as the fusion procedure but its rules are deferred to the sibling pace `apcs-combining-algorithm`.

Apply these decisions to `Tools/apck/APCS0-SpecTop.adoc`. No code changes. No decisions re-opened.

## Prerequisite
Both container memos complete:
- `Memos/memo-20260420-apck-container-fixture-assay/` — fixture assay with observed complementarity and failure modes (from ₢A9AAZ)
- `Memos/memo-20260420-apck-container-discerner-fields.md` — authoritative per-field semantics with library citations (from ₢A9AAf)
## Working primitives (from mount-and-design conversation)
These are the architectural shapes committed to during the mount conversation — to be pressure-tested, refined, and formalized into APCS0 during remount:
### 1. Phrase-anchor propagation (two-phase combining)
Combining is not only per-span aggregation. It is also *using* container entity findings to drive search into the input text:

- **Anchor phase.** Select phrase-level entity findings from container discerners that meet a quality gate (parameter — source TBD: Stanford-only, Stanford + Stanza corroborated, or any container entity).

- **Verbatim expansion.** For each anchor phrase, mark every verbatim occurrence in the input text as PHI, inheriting the anchor's category. Handles "Margaret J. Thornton introduced once, later referenced as Thornton."

- **Word decomposition + propagation.** Split each anchor phrase into word tokens; for each word, find input-text occurrences gated by: word-boundary match, case-match or capitalization, absence from suppression whitelists (English, medical), optional POS gate (PROPN).
- **Category inheritance.** Propagated spans take the anchor's category.
### 2. Consistent pseudonymization via entity clusters
Committed: anonymized output uses stable pseudonyms (`[HCW-A]`, `[HCW-B]`, `[FAMILY-A]`, `[FACILITY-A]`, etc.) rather than pure redaction.
- Combining must produce **entity clusters**, not just findings. "Dr. Chen" / "Dr. Susan Chen" / "Chen" all resolve to one cluster → one pseudonym.
- Preserves referential structure for downstream reasoning (Open Evidence's "provider 1 vs provider 2 disagreed").
- Safe-Harbor-compatible as long as labels are not reversible to the individual.
- Cluster-forming primitive is distinct from both propagation and categorization — requires its own architectural treatment.
### 3. scispaCy POS as homograph gate
- scispaCy's generic `ENTITY` label is weight-zero for category decisions (confirmed: MedMentions mention-detector design consequence, not a defect).

- scispaCy tokens' `pos` (UPOS) earn their keep as the homograph disambiguator for word-level propagation: PROPN → propagate; AUX / VERB / common NOUN → suppress. Resolves "May" (name, PROPN) vs "may" (modal, AUX).
### 4. Rust-side dictionaries as propagation suppressors

- `apcsds_english` and `apcsds_medical` whitelists serve as *suppressors* during word-level propagation, not just as independent detection signals. First-name homographs like "Hope," "May," "April," "Faith," "Hunter" are the motivating case.
- `apcsds_firstname` / `apcsds_surname` / `apcsds_city` remain independent detection + corroboration signals for the anchor phase.
## Deferred architectural sub-decisions (resolve in remount)
- **Anchor source.** Stanford-only (confidence self-report), Stanford + Stanza corroborated (two-signal gate), or any container entity finding.
- **Cluster output shape.** Cluster membership first-class on each `apcs_unified_finding`, vs. post-combining pass on the finding list.
- **Propagation scope per category.** PATIENT, HCW, FACILITY, LOCALITY — which categories get aggressive word-level propagation, and which stay phrase-only.
- **Category enum.** `apcs_phi_category` — ADDRESS / FACILITY / LOCALITY as distinct values, or a single "place-ish PHI" with disposition-layer distinction.
- **Pseudonym namespace scope.** HCW cluster labels separate from PATIENT cluster labels separate from FACILITY cluster labels, vs. unified labels.
## Questions to answer (reshaped from original nine)
Reframed as process-and-primitive questions, not parameter-picking:

1. **HOSPITAL / ADDRESS / GPE triangulation rule** — name the rule combining Stanford HOSPITAL + Stanza FAC/GPE + regex address shape into distinct `apcs_phi_category` values. Signals, ordering, tie-breakers.
2. **Structured-ID precedence** — name the "regex overrides model fragmentation at the same span" rule as a combining primitive (SSN, encounter IDs, email, URL).
3. **spaCy entity utility** — *partly resolved*: entities weight-zero; tokens serve as POS gate. Remaining: are spaCy entity spans *ever* primary signal, or strictly suppressed?
4. **Dictionary + model corroboration** — how `apcsds_surname/firstname/city` compose with Stanford PATIENT / Stanza PERSON. Sharpened by dictionaries-as-propagation-suppressors primitive.
5. **Confidence-gate placement** (reframed from "pick a threshold") — name the extension point where Stanford confidence is consulted. Numeric parameter deferred.
6. **Granularity** — *partly resolved* by phrase-anchor propagation: phrase-scope anchors + word-scope expansion. Remaining: edge cases (partial spans, overlapping anchors).
7. **Clinician toggle composition** — where toggles compose with clusters and categories (discerner-level, post-reconciliation mutator, disposition override).
8. **VENDOR label** — Safe Harbor question flagged to APCAS; not resolved here.

9. **Field-level enquoining discipline** — which finding-record fields earn quoins in APCS0, driven by which fields the combining rules actually reference. Field-reference memo has pre-done half the thinking (identified redundancies: Stanza `ner` vs entity variant, spaCy `tag NNP` vs `pos PROPN`).
## Deliverables

## Character

Two orthogonal observability improvements combined because they're cheap, both informed by one debugging session, and together they produce the evidence the next diagnostic pace needs. Low ambiguity on the timestamp side; the save-everything side has one naming decision (tag-in-filename) and a posture note to pin down.
## Problem
Two evidence gaps surfaced in today's fixture-load session on the real device:

1. **No clock on the log.** Log lines carry `[LEVEL] [file:line]` but no time. Correlating events across runs, or with Ann's "it stopped working around 2pm" kind of report, is currently impossible without re-running under a stopwatch.

2. **Classification failure erases the evidence.** A 26461-byte Epic copy arrived on the clipboard and was classified non-clinical. No harvest was written because harvest is gated behind the Clinical branch. We have exactly three data points — byte count, content type label, and "it didn't match" — and zero bytes of the actual capture to post-mortem. Meanwhile pasting the same clipboard into Microsoft Word retained formatting, which is direct evidence that the HTML flavor *was* on the clipboard and that the classifier isn't seeing everything arboard could hand it.
The classifier bug itself is out of scope here. This pace is about ensuring the *next* failure leaves an artifact on disk.
## Solution
Two independent deliverables that land together:

1. **Timestamp every log entry.** Format becomes `[LEVEL] [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm] [file:line] message`. Single format discipline — stdout and tee go through the same formatter, no drift. `chrono` is already a dep (used in the DOB age transform).

2. **Always harvest, tag by classification.** Lift the harvest call out of the Clinical arm of `zapcap_handle_focus` so every focus with changed content captures the text + HTML flavors to the journal. The filename carries a classification tag: `{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` or `{N}-in.nonclinical.{ext}`. Anonymized output remains Clinical-only and keeps its current name `{N}-out.txt`.
## Deliverables — Code

- `apcrl_log.rs`: add `chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S%.3f")` between the level and the `file:line` sections. One formatter reached by both the stdout path and the tee sink. If any `apcrl_*` tests assert on format, update them.
- `apcrh_harvest.rs`:
- Writers emit `{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` or `{N}-in.nonclinical.{ext}` depending on a classification tag argument. Suggested enum or `&str` — Brad's call during execution.
- Scanner already parses the leading digit run and handles arbitrary infixes — no change needed.
- Doc comment updated for the new shape.
- `apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:
- Reorder so classification runs first, then harvest is called unconditionally with the resolved tag.
- Clinical arm still writes `{N}-out.txt` after `Ok(n)`.
- Same-as-last short-circuit stays at the top — we don't want to re-harvest the identical clipboard on every focus oscillation.
- `clipboard.set_text(String::new())` (the zero-out) stays Clinical-only. Non-clinical content is not consumed.
- `apcth_harvest.rs`:
- Update 4 existing tests to new naming.
- Add one test covering both `.clinical.` and `.nonclinical.` tagged files scanning correctly and sharing index space.
## Deliverables — Spec (APCPS)
- **Observability Log → Format paragraph**: update to show the timestamp prefix.
- **Clipboard Harvest → Trigger paragraph**: changes from "Clinical branch" to "every focus with content change".
- **Clipboard Harvest → Naming paragraph**: describe `{N}-in.clinical.{ext}` vs `{N}-in.nonclinical.{ext}` and note that the leading-digit-run scanner continues to unify the index space.

- **Clipboard Harvest → Privacy posture paragraph**: expansion note. Today's compact is "clipbuddy sees clinical clipboards". The new compact is "clipbuddy captures every clipboard it sees during a session". Journal stays on-device — no new *kind* of risk — but the *scope* of what Brad observes when reviewing Ann's sessions grows to include her passwords, URLs, personal email, anything she copies. Name this explicitly so the posture is not discovered by surprise.
- **Journal Directory → Contents table**: add a row for `{N}-in.nonclinical.{ext}` or reshape the existing Harvest captures row to cover both tags.
## Constraints
- Log format single-source-of-truth — stdout and tee never drift.
- Scanner logic unchanged — leading-digit parse already tolerates arbitrary infixes.
- Harvest failure remains non-fatal.
- System clipboard is zeroed only on the Clinical branch. Non-clinical captures do not disturb Ann's clipboard state.
- The classification tag is decided *before* harvest is called, not inferred from filename later.
## Exit Criteria

- Focus on a **clinical** clipboard produces `{N}-in.clinical.txt`, `{N}-in.clinical.html`, and `{N}-out.txt`.
- Re-focusing with unchanged content does not produce duplicate captures (`focus: unchanged` short-circuit preserved).
- APCPS Observability Log, Clipboard Harvest, and Journal Directory sections updated and proofread; privacy posture expansion note written in prose, not a bullet list.
## Out of Scope

## Character

Exploratory research, not implementation. This pace produces a **docket**, not shipped code. Brad runs the existing detector against the flavors captured by the preceding pace, observes where it succeeds and fails, and emerges with a defended choice: "classify from X by method Y." Cognitive posture is empirical/comparative — resist the urge to implement a winning path inside this pace; the conclusion is input to the *next* pace.
## Problem

After the preceding pace lands, the journal directory holds per-flavor captures for every Epic Copy All. The classifier currently reads only UTF-8 plain text (`clipboard.get_text()` via arboard), and that path classified a 26461-byte Epic copy as non-clinical because the clinical heuristic depends on structural cues (labels, section headers) that the UTF-8 flavor strips. RTF carries vastly more structure — 139559 bytes vs 26461 on Ann's captured session — but isn't HTML, and our existing parser (`apcrp_parse.rs`) assumes HTML.
Three plausible paths exist, each with different integration cost and different failure modes:
1. **Classify from RTF directly** — add an RTF parser, extract labels/sections, feed Tier 2/3.
2. **RTF → HTML pre-pass** — convert, keep `apcrp_parse.rs` unchanged. Lowest structural risk, but adds conversion artifacts.
3. **Enrich the UTF-8 text pipeline** — strengthen Tier 2 regex anchors, accept that some Epic structure is lost. Cheapest.
Without running the detector against real captures, we can't tell which path's tradeoffs are acceptable. Committing to one by argument alone repeats the mistake of the ₢A9AAW premise.
## Solution

Hands-on comparison using real captures from Ann's journal. Brad writes a throwaway `apcab`-style scratch binary that takes a flavor file + format hint and runs the detector, emitting a comparable finding report. Runs the detector against RTF, UTF-8, and (if applicable) UTF-16 from the same capture. Judges:
- **Recall** — which flavor's findings best match the known PHI in that capture?
- **Precision** — does any flavor introduce new false positives?
- **Integration cost** — what does it take to actually wire the winning flavor into the main pipeline?
Writes a comparison report and a successor pace docket with concrete deliverables.
## Deliverables

- Throwaway flavor-evaluation binary. May live outside the main tree (`apck/apcd/src/apcxx_scratch.rs` or similar, excluded from `cargo test`). Reads a captured file, asks the detector for findings, writes a report to stdout or a text file.
- Comparison notes — brief, one paragraph per candidate path (RTF-direct / RTF→HTML / enrich-UTF-8). Must include a rejection rationale for the unchosen paths, not just a selection.
- A new pace docket describing the chosen path with deliverables, constraints, exit criteria. Slated into this heat, ready for execution.
## Constraints

- **Real captures only.** Synthetic fixtures don't tell us what actually-delivered RTF looks like. At least one real Epic Copy All with known PHI — Ann clicks through a test encounter in her sandbox, Brad reviews.
- **Evaluation binary is disposable.** No tests, no polish, no commit churn. Output is the docket, not the binary.
- **Rejection rationales are load-bearing.** "We picked X because Y, and rejected Z because W" — not just "we picked X." Future you will thank present you.
- **No detector changes in this pace.** Tuning dictionaries, regex, or severity merging is orthogonal and belongs elsewhere.
## Exit Criteria
- Two or more real Epic captures processed through the evaluation binary; per-flavor finding reports on disk.
- Comparison notes written, arguing for the winning path.
- New implementation pace slated into this heat with a full docket ready for mount.
- Evaluation scratch code archived, deleted, or clearly marked disposable.
## Out of Scope
- Implementing the chosen detection path. That's the docket-that-emerges-from-this-pace's job.
- Shipping the evaluation binary. It's scaffolding.
- Classifier tuning (dictionaries, regex, severity merging) — orthogonal.
- RTF parser selection as a permanent Cargo dep — if the winning path is RTF-direct, dep selection is part of the implementation pace, not this one.

## Character

Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
## Prerequisites
A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
## Context

Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
## Scope — Container Source
Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:

- `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
- `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
- `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
- Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
- Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
- On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
- Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
- Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
- Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).

- `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).

- `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).

- `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
## Scope — Tabtargets
Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
- `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`

- `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
- `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
- `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
## Scope — Smoke Test
After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:

1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.

3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
## Constraints

- **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.

- **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
- **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.

- `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir: &Path, index: u64, timeout: Duration) -> Result<DiscernerReply>`: `notify` crate (FSEvents on macOS) watches for `{index}.json`; reads + deserializes on appearance; informative error on timeout or deserialize failure
- `apcrc_ping(journal_dir: &Path, timeout: Duration) -> Result<PingReply>`: ping request/reply round-trip
**Modify `apcd/src/apcrh_harvest.rs`:**

- Non-Clinical: no request file

**Modify `apcd/src/apcap_main.rs::zapcap_handle_focus`:**
- After harvest + anonymized-output write, spawn non-blocking task calling `apcrc_await_output(journal_dir, N, timeout)`
- Success log: `container output received for N: stanford=S spacy_tok=T spacy_ent=E stanza_tok=T stanza_ent=E`
- Timeout/error: `[WARN] container output not received within {timeout} for index N: {error}`

Formalize the Envelope component of Vesture in VLS.
## Context
Envelope is the fourth component of Vesture (alongside signet_case, separator, epithet_case). Deferred during VOS development because the dual-nature (suffix vs frame) was blocking progress.
**Post VLS/BUS extraction:** Vesture definitions now live in VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc, not VOS.
## Current State
Domain vestures in VLS already specify envelope values informally:
- Rust Vesture: "envelope: none"
- Bash Vesture: "envelope: `.sh` for files"
- AsciiDoc Vesture: "envelope: none (attributes), `[[` `]]` for anchors"
- Publication Vesture: "envelope: `.adoc`, `.md`"
- Git Vesture: "envelope: none"
- Slash Command Vesture: "envelope: `.md` for files"
## Issues to Resolve
1. **Two flavors exist:**
- Suffix envelope: `.sh`, `.rs`, `.adoc`
- One concept with sub-types.
2. **Prefix vs suffix vs frame:**
- Slash commands: `/` prefix
- Git refs: `refs/` prefix
- Extensions: `.sh` suffix
- Frames: both sides (`:...:`), `[[...]]`
3. **Function invocation:** `()` is invocation syntax, not envelope.
## Proposed Resolution
Envelope is polymorphic with three sub-types:
- **Suffix**: appended (`.sh`, `.adoc`)
- **Prefix**: prepended (`/`, `refs/`)
## Implementation
1. Add `[[voslve_envelope]]` definition to VLS Core Liturgy Terms section
2. Define the three sub-types in the definition
3. Update `[[voslv_vesture]]` to reference the new term
4. Vesture domains already have values - formalize the pattern
## Target File
Tools/vok/vov_veiled/VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc

Establish AXLA-compliant linked term patterns for state machines and their state values. JJSA's pace states serve as the exemplar; pattern should generalize.
## Context

JJSA defines pace states (`rough`, `bridled`, `complete`, `abandoned`) with `jjdpe_*` prefix and heat status (`current`, `retired`) with `jjdhe_*` prefix. These voice `axt_enum_value`, which is correct but incomplete:
1. **No machine identity**: The state machine itself has no linked term — only its values do
2. **Implicit ensign**: The `pe` in `jjdpe_` implicitly means "pace enum" but this isn't documented
3. **No transition semantics**: State machines have allowed transitions; these aren't captured
4. **AXLA gap**: No motif for "state machine" as an entity type distinct from plain enumeration
## Proposed Pattern
**State Machine Entity**: An `axo_entity` with named identity, enumerated values, optional transition rules.
**Naming Convention (using liturgy from VLS)**:
- Machine: `jjdsm_{name}` — `sm` ensign meaning "state machine"
- Values: `jjd{abbrev}e_{value}` — `{abbrev}` identifies which machine, `e` = enum value
## AXLA Additions
Add `axsm_*` category for state machine motifs:
- axsm_state_machine, axsm_state_value, axsm_transition
- axsm_initial, axsm_terminal
## JJSA Updates
Add state machine definitions and update state value definitions to reference their machine.
## VLS Update (optional)
Document `sm` ensign in VLS liturgy section as the standard for state machines.
## Deliverables
1. AXLA: Add `axsm_*` category for state machine motifs
2. JJSA: Add `jjdsm_pace_lifecycle` and `jjdsm_heat_status` entities
3. JJSA: Update `jjdpe_*` and `jjdhe_*` definitions to voice `axsm_state_value`
4. VLS (optional): Document `sm` ensign in Ensign examples
End-to-end review: VOS/VLS/BUS specifications versus Rust/Bash implementation reality.
## Purpose
After MVP + Whisper-lite implementation + VLS/BUS extraction, assess the gap between specifications (the plan) and what we built.
## Post-Extraction Context
VOS was refactored:
- **VLS** (Vox Liturgical Specification): Universal naming vocabulary extracted from VOS
- **BUS** (Bash Utilities Specification): Tabtarget dispatch vocabulary extracted from VOS
- **VOS** retains: kit distribution operations, entities, assets, control terms
This reconciliation covers all three specs.
## Two Failure Modes
### 1. Unrecognized Complexity
Things harder than specs suggested:
- **Layered architecture** — VOS didn't clearly separate bash orchestration from Rust utilities
- **Template lifecycle** — not specified how templates flow from vov_veiled → parcel → target
- **Naming collisions** — "install" meant different things at different layers
### 2. Overspecification Without Quality
VOS prose that didn't translate to better code:
- **Builder API** — Fluent pattern specified; static structs work fine
- **Runtime Conclave** — implied runtime registration; compile-time is simpler
- **Liturgy depth** — now in VLS; useful for thinking, or baroque naming?
## Review Process
1. **Implementation Inventory** — list what exists after MVP
2. **Section Audit** — classify each VOS/VLS/BUS section as: implemented as specified, differently, not implemented, or unspecified
3. **Gap Analysis** — for each gap: VOS wrong, expedient, or emergent?
4. **Revision** — update specs to match reality
5. **Lessons Learned** — document what worked and what didn't
## Deliverables
1. Gap analysis document
2. Revised VOS/VLS/BUS reflecting implementation reality
3. Steeplechase entry with lessons learned
Implement prefix tree registry in vof with vvx checker utility, and tighten concept model documents.
## Problem
Prefix tree integrity across codebase. Need to ensure all inscriptions use registered prefixes and respect terminal exclusivity.
## Approach
Bake all known prefix tree paths into vof. VVX provides `vvx check` utility that scans repo for prefix violations.
### Registry Enhancement
Extend vof beyond cipher registry to full prefix tree:
- Terminal prefixes (leaf nodes that name things): `buc_`, `jjrg_`
- Non-terminal prefixes (have children): `bu`, `jjr`
- Tree structure with parent-child relationships
### Checker Utility
`vvx check` subcommand:
1. Scan all files in repo (respecting .gitignore)

2. **BUS**: Dispatch patterns documented; vof validates tabtarget prefixes.
3. **CLAUDE.md**: Trim "Prefix Naming Discipline" — point to `vvx check` instead.
4. **Other concept models**: Remove redundant prefix documentation.

## Deliverables

2. `vvx check` command implementation
3. Simplified VLS/CLAUDE.md prefix documentation

- Location: `Tools/vok/src/vocv_conclave.rs`
- Build-time: collects all Whisper declarations
- Provides: kit enumeration, template paths, concept model paths
**Concept model registration** — Foundation for future acronym validation:
- Each kit registers its concept models (JJSA, VOS, MCM, etc.)
- Conclave can enumerate all concept models across kits
- Future: validate acronym uniqueness, build cross-reference index
## What We Simplify
**No builder API** — Static struct, not fluent builder:
```rust
// Instead of Whisper::for_kit("jjk").cipher(JJ).display_name(...)
pub const JJK_WHISPER: vofw_Whisper = vofw_Whisper {
kit_id: "jjk",
cipher: &JJ,
display_name: "Job Jockey",
managed_sections: &[...],
concept_models: &["JJS0-GallopsData.adoc"],
**No runtime registration** — Compile-time const aggregation:
```rust
// In vocv_conclave.rs
pub const ALL_WHISPERS: &[&vofw_Whisper] = &[
&buk::BUK_WHISPER,
&cmk::CMK_WHISPER,
&jjk::JJK_WHISPER,
&vvk::VVK_WHISPER,
## VOS Updates
1. **Simplify vose_whisper** — Replace builder API example with static struct
2. **Simplify vose_conclave** — Remove "collects at runtime" language, clarify compile-time
3. **Keep concept model registration** — Valuable for future tooling
4. **Remove .register(&mut conclave)** — Not needed with static aggregation
## Implementation
### 1. Create Whisper type (vof)
```rust
// Tools/vok/vof/src/vofw_whisper.rs
pub struct vofw_Whisper {
pub kit_id: &'static str,
pub cipher: &'static vofc_Cipher,
pub display_name: &'static str,
pub managed_sections: &'static [vofc_ManagedSection],
pub concept_models: &'static [&'static str],
### 2. Create Whisper for each kit
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/vowbuw_whisper.rs — BUK declaration
- Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/vowcmw_whisper.rs — CMK declaration
- Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/vowjjw_whisper.rs — JJK declaration
- Tools/vvk/vov_veiled/vowvvw_whisper.rs — VVK declaration
### 3. Create Conclave (vok)
```rust
// Tools/vok/src/vocv_conclave.rs
pub const ALL_WHISPERS: &[&vofw_Whisper] = &[...];
pub fn vocv_kits() -> impl Iterator<Item = &'static vofw_Whisper>;
pub fn vocv_managed_sections() -> impl Iterator<Item = (&'static str, &'static vofc_ManagedSection)>;
pub fn vocv_concept_models() -> impl Iterator<Item = (&'static str, &'static str)>;
### 4. Migrate callers
- release_collect: use vocv_kits() instead of DISTRIBUTABLE_KITS
- vvx_emplace: use vocv_managed_sections()
- vvx_vacate: use vocv_managed_sections() for tags
### 5. Deprecate centralized registry
- Remove DISTRIBUTABLE_KITS from vofc_registry.rs
- Keep vofc_Kit and vofc_ManagedSection types (used by Whisper)
- Update any remaining callers
## Benefits
- **Decentralized ownership** — Kit authors edit their own Whisper
- **Concept model foundation** — Ready for acronym validation tooling
- **Simpler than VOS spec** — Static structs, compile-time aggregation
- **Type-safe** — Compiler catches missing Whispers

- ₢AAABL (test-mvp) — Validates MVP before we refactor

- All existing tests pass
- Release/install/uninstall cycle still works
- Each kit has its own Whisper file

Implement trophy computation: sessions, timelines, flights.
## Session Clustering
Input: steeplechase entries with timestamps
Algorithm:
1. Sort entries by timestamp
2. Group entries where gap < 30 minutes = same session
3. Compute duration from first to last entry in session
## Timeline Extraction
Input: pace tacks array
Output: compact timeline of state transitions
Algorithm:
1. Walk tacks in reverse (oldest first)
2. Extract {ts, state, agent} for each state change
3. agent field populated if state is "bridled"
## Flight Record Building
Input: steeplechase entries (B, F, L, W markers)
Output: flight records per pace
Algorithm:
1. Find B marker for pace → flight start setup
2. Find F marker → execution began
3. Find L marker → execution ended, capture result
4. Correlate files from L marker body
## Implementation
- New module jjrt_trophy.rs or functions in jjro_ops.rs
- Pure functions: steeplechase → sessions, tacks → timeline, markers → flights

Iterate on minimum viable CLAUDE.md cue to get models to format CSV as a table.
## Approach

Build a trivial `jjx_table` command that echoes CSV stdin back as raw CSV output. Then run controlled trials across model tiers and cue levels to find the simplest cue that reliably produces formatted tables.
## Step 1: Build jjx_table
Minimal Rust command:
- Reads CSV lines from stdin
- Echoes them back unchanged to stdout
- No formatting, no parsing — just a passthrough
Files: new command file in Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/, register in jjrx_cli.rs
## Step 2: Define cue levels
| Level | CLAUDE.md Cue |
| 0 | Nothing — no cue at all |
| 1 | "jjx_table outputs CSV. Present as a table." |
| 2 | "When jjx commands emit CSV, render as markdown table for the user." |
| 3 | Full slash command with step-by-step instructions (control/baseline) |
## Step 3: Run trials
For each cue level, test across model tiers using Task tool with model param:
- Opus: Task(model="opus")
- Sonnet: Task(model="sonnet")
- Haiku: Task(model="haiku")
Each trial: call jjx_table with 3-5 CSV rows, capture model's response, grade pass/fail (did it produce a markdown table with correct values?).
Test CSV payload (fixed across trials):
Name,Status,Count
alpha,racing,5
beta,stabled,12
gamma,retired,8
## Step 4: Record results
Build a 4x3 matrix (cue level x model tier) in a memo documenting:
- Pass/fail per cell
- Qualitative notes (added headers? extra commentary? reformatted values?)
- Minimum viable cue level per tier
## Step 5: Iterate
If Level 2 works universally, that's the target cue for CLAUDE.md.
If tiers diverge, consider whether --model flag lets jjx pre-format for weaker models.
## Success Criteria
- jjx_table command builds and runs
- At least 3 trials per cell in the matrix
- Clear recommendation for minimum cue wording
- Memo with findings: Memos/memo-YYYYMMDD-cue-calibration-csv-table.md
Spook fix: jjx_transfer (restring) fails silently through MCP.
## Two bugs
1. **CLAUDE.md says coronets is newline-separated string** but jjrrs_restring.rs:45 deserializes via `serde_json::from_str::<Vec<String>>()` — needs a JSON array like `["AYAAA"]`.
2. **Errors go to stderr, not buf** — all error paths in jjrrs_run() use `eprintln!()` and return `(1, empty_buf)`. MCP transport only sees buf, so errors are silently lost.
## Fix
- Update CLAUDE.md (and vocjjmc_core.md) to document coronets as JSON array
- Change `eprintln!` error paths in jjrrs_restring.rs to `write!(buf, ...)` so errors flow through MCP
- Audit other commands for same eprintln pattern

Why this pace exists: The data model paces define types. The execution paces define mechanics. The command surface is the user-facing contract.
What this pace produces -- JJS0 sections covering:

1. **New command specs**: school (reads docket + codebase + gait piaffes, produces beat table entries with nosebag flow), breeze (triggers chukker-sequenced dispatch with provender collection/nosebag routing), corral (per-pace review with provender visible for context), longe (heat-level readiness), tackle CRUD, gait management commands.

3. **Phase boundary commands** -- what jjx validates at each transition: pre-school blaze/chain checks, pre-breeze beat table integrity (including nosebag expectation satisfaction), post-corral volte completion.

6. **Provender inspection** -- how users view provender and nosebag content for debugging/understanding beat output. Read-only visibility into the informational flow.

## Scope
This pace produces JJS0 spec sections for the tackle/blaze system:
1. **jjrg_Tackle type definition** — silks-identified, mutable, contains ₿-named file scopes, actions (command + exclusivity), gait affinities, read-also entries
2. **Blaze (₿) mechanics** — definition ownership (tackle defines, gaits/dockets reference), uniqueness scope (per-heat), validation rules (no duplicates, no orphan references)
3. **Tackle-gait interface contract** — how gaits declare required ₿ blazes, how jjx validates compatibility at school time

4. **Tackle integrity declarations** — build commands, test runs, linters, and quality checks that a tackle makes available. These inform school's chukker boundary decisions: whether mechanical git merge with tackle-declared checks suffices, or explicit alignment beats are needed.
5. **Exclusivity scopes** — jjee_ enum (worktree/workstation/project), how jjx enforces mutexes during concurrent chukker dispatch
6. **Top-level gallops key** — tackles registry alongside gaits registry
## Design constraints
- Tackles decouple dockets from concrete paths — school resolves blazes when writing per-beat warrant fields in the gallops beat table
- Blaze is NOT a global identity (not in ₣/₢/₡/Ꝗ table) — scoped to tackle, resolved at school time
- Paddock tackle/blaze vocabulary is authoritative starting point

Scope: This pace produces JJS0 spec sections for the gait system:

1. **jjrg_Gait record fields** -- silks (shared across versions), default model preferences, confidence gates, required blazes. Beat templates carry a **piaffe** -- the named prose field declaring purpose, approach, nosebag production/expectations, and resources.

2. **Piaffe structure and nosebag declarations** -- piaffe contains: purpose, approach, nosebag production (sections this beat emits in provender), nosebag expectations (nosebag names needed from prior chukkers), resources (file scopes, blaze references). Also: auto-tool declarations and quality requirements.
3. **Nosebag routing contract** -- how piaffes declare what nosebags each beat produces/expects for jjx to match headers at chukker boundaries. Name-matched, not ID-matched.
4. **Chukker assignment** -- beat templates carry chukker numbers. Same-chukker beats concurrent; chukkers sequential. School inherits chukker structure from gait.
5. **Merge/alignment beats** -- gaits CAN name alignment beats at chukker boundaries. Not required. Tackle integrity declarations inform need.
6. **Quirt identity** -- allocation strategy, next_quirt seed, immutability.
7. **Gaits registry** -- top-level gallops key, keyed by quirt.
Design constraints:
- Gaits are school-time resources, not breeze-time
- Immutable snapshots; evolution mints new quirts with same silks
- Abstract beat (template+piaffe) vs concrete beat (warrant+provender) -- context disambiguates
- Docket to piaffe to warrant: intent to template to concrete (prose field escalation)
- Paddock authoritative (checklists for school, confidence gates)
